reasoning: Peer set (LEFT) consists of standalone equipment icons — helmets and shields — rendered in cool metallic grays, blues, and golds with bright specular highlights on curved surfaces. Candidate (RIGHT) shows leather armor WORN on a character torso, immediately breaking the standalone-icon presentation convention. Outline weight (1px) and outline color (near-black) are consistent with peers. Shadow direction (top-left light source) also matches. However: (1) Color temperature is warm brown/orange on pauldrons + green-teal on chest vs the cool-dominant metallic palette of peers — notable divergence. (2) Highlight style is matte/fabric rather than the metallic specular blobs seen on peer helmets/shields — expected for leather vs metal, but still reduces family cohesion. (3) Palette overlap is shallow: shared only in outline and shadow; no primary-color overlap. (4) The full-figure armor presentation vs isolated equipment icon breaks silhouette consistency. (5) Charm level: peers read as crisp, iconic items; candidate blends item + character form, diluting the iconic quality. Rendering technique (pixel art, anti-alias level, detail density) is consistent. Score 6 — passes technical rendering bar but presentation and palette choices create visible seam vs the peer family.
